aronui.when_to_use: When you have an HTML report that may contain empty or too-short sections and you want to automatically regenerate them to meet length requirements.
aronui.output_shape: String containing the repaired HTML content.

pitfalls.text: Missing required 'content' parameter results in empty output 'success: ' with no error.
Providing non-HTML content (e.g., a file path or plain text) returns that content unchanged without any error or repair.
Invalid language values (e.g., 'es') are silently accepted; the tool proceeds without error.

procedure: Provide the required 'content' parameter with a valid HTML string.
Optionally provide 'topic' to override the inferred topic (inferred from <h1> if missing).
Optionally set 'language' to 'de' or 'en' for language-specific regeneration (validation is not strict).
The tool scans each section of the HTML report; if a section is empty or has fewer words than min_words_target * min_words_ok_ratio, it is regenerated.
The output is a repaired HTML string (or unchanged if no repair was needed).
verification: Ensure the output is a string; if content was provided, it should be non-empty (unless content was empty).
If the input had empty or too-short sections, verify that those sections have been expanded in the output.
If content is missing, the output will be an empty string (no error).
If content is valid HTML, the output should be structurally valid HTML.

predict_records.intent:
{"content": "

Test Topic

This is a sample paragraph that contains enough words to demonstrate the repair functionality. The tool will check if any sections are empty or too short
predict_records.predicted: success: The tool will parse the HTML, infer the topic from the h1, evaluate the body section, and since the paragraph has sufficient length, return a repaired version (likely unchanged or with minor fixes).
predict_records.actual:
success:

Test Topic

This is a sample paragraph that contains enough words to demonstrate the repair functionality. The tool will check if any sections are empty or too short and regenera

predict_records.predicted: error: The tool will return an error because the language parameter expects one of 'de' or 'en', but 'es' is provided.
error: The tool expects HTML content; providing a file path (not containing HTML tags) will likely cause a parsing failure or error.

predict_records.intent: {"content": "This is a seed file.\nIt has some text.\nMore text."}
predict_records.predicted: error: The tool will error because the content is not valid HTML (no tags).
predict_records.actual:
success: This is a seed file.
It has some text.
More text.

predict_records.predicted: error: The tool will raise an error because the required parameter 'content' is missing.
predict_records.judge_reason: Agent predicted an error due to missing parameter, but actual response was empty, indicating no error occurred.

predict_records.predicted: success: The tool will return the repaired HTML (likely unchanged since content has enough words).
predict_records.judge_reason: The agent predicted success and the tool returned a valid response (ellipsis likely indicates successful output).

predict_records.predicted: error: Missing required parameter 'content' will cause a validation error or TypeError.
predict_records.judge_reason: The agent predicted a missing 'content' parameter error, and the tool returned that same validation error.
